audit_items.proof_trace: The closure library (MethodExtClosure_* and FunctionExtClosure_*) deliberately omits null checks on stored object/function pointers before dereferencing them in Run(), following a standard C++ callback contract where callers are responsible for non-null inputs. No concrete attack path exists from network RPC input to passing a null pointer to NewExtClosure/NewPermanentExtClosure: all factory call-sites use &ClassName::method (address-of, never null) or shared_from_this() (throws on invalid use, doesn't return null), and network-supplied data never flows into the object/function arguments of the factory functions.
